context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM calls across multiple providers (OpenAI, Cohere) to enable cost monitoring by extracting usage metadata from API responses.
key_requirements: Extract input/output token counts from OpenAI and Cohere API responses in provider implementations
Integrate with existing instrumentation framework to track LLM method calls via BenchmarkHook protocol
Store token usage data in Answer.metadata for visibility to end users
constraints: Only implement token counting for providers that expose usage metadata natively (OpenAI, Cohere)
Maintain backward compatibility with existing Answer data structure
Avoid performance impact from token counting logic (minimal overhead)
stakeholders: user requesting token usage tracking to see how many LLM tokens each query costs

in_scope: Add token usage tracking for LLM queries
Extract token counts from API responses where available (OpenAI, Cohere)
Extend Answer metadata with token cost information
Instrument chat methods in LLM providers
out_of_scope: Per-token breakdowns or detailed usage analytics
Token counting for Anthropic and Ollama providers without native API support
Implementation of custom token counting logic for providers that don't expose usage data
Modification of Answer class to support arbitrary metadata fields beyond token cost

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/answer.py — Answer dataclass for paradigm-agnostic answer representation
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_tracker.py — Main token tracking implementation using instrumentation hooks
fitz_ai/core/answer.py — Extend Answer to include token usage data in metadata
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — Update OpenAI provider with token counting
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Update Anthropic provider with token counting
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Update Cohere provider with token counting
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Update Ollama provider with token counting

assumptions.assumption: Token counting will be extracted from API responses where providers return usage metadata
assumptions.impact: Need to implement custom counting logic or rely on external services if providers don't expose usage data

assumptions.assumption: The instrumentation framework already supports tracking method calls with context
assumptions.impact: May need to modify the InstrumentedProxy implementation if it doesn't provide sufficient context

assumptions.assumption: Token counting can be done at provider level without complex integration points
assumptions.impact: Need to instrument higher-level engine calls instead of individual providers if provider interfaces are insufficient

assumptions.assumption: The main LLM chat methods (chat) and embedding methods are the primary cost drivers
assumptions.impact: May miss important operations in query pipeline such as reranking or vision processing that also contribute to token usage

assumptions.assumption: Token usage data will be stored in Answer.metadata for visibility to end users
assumptions.impact: Need alternative approach like separate logging or metrics system if the existing Answer class doesn't support arbitrary metadata fields

approaches.name: Instrumentation Hook with Provider Interface Extension
approaches.description: Extend LLM provider interfaces to return full API responses alongside text results, then modify instrumentation hooks to parse token usage from these extended responses.
pros: Maintains clean separation of concerns between providers and token tracking logic
Enables comprehensive token counting for all supported providers
Follows existing codebase patterns with protocol extensions
Allows graceful handling of missing or malformed usage data
cons: Requires modification to multiple provider interfaces
Changes public API surface area of LLM providers
Adds complexity to provider implementation layer
best_for: Production systems requiring accurate token tracking
Scalable architectures where performance is critical

approaches.name: HTTP Client Level Instrumentation
approaches.description: Instrument at the HTTP client level (e.g., wrapping httpx.Client) to capture raw API responses before they reach provider methods, then pass this data through to hooks.
pros: No changes needed to existing LLM provider interfaces
Captures token usage directly from source responses
Maintains backward compatibility with current APIs
cons: More complex architectural change requiring HTTP-level instrumentation
Potential performance overhead from additional wrapping layers
May interfere with existing connection pooling or retry mechanisms
best_for: Systems where provider interfaces cannot be modified
Architectures needing minimal impact on existing codebase

architecture.recommended: Instrumentation Hook with Provider Interface Extension
architecture.reasoning: This approach is right because it directly addresses the root cause identified in the analysis: LLM providers only expose final answer strings, not raw API responses containing token usage data. The recommended approach solves this by extending provider interfaces to optionally return full responses alongside text answers (chat_with_response method), allowing hooks like TokenTrackerHook to access and parse actual usage data from response objects rather than relying on post-processing of Answer objects which lose the token information during transformation. This is better than HTTP client instrumentation because it follows existing patterns in the codebase, maintains clean separation between concerns, and requires minimal changes to the core engine while enabling comprehensive token tracking support for all providers that expose usage metadata.

key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_depth_vs_performance: The system chooses to only track tokens when explicitly opted-in via instrumentation hooks, avoiding performance impact for users who don't need token tracking. However, this requires restructuring the hook system to capture raw API responses which is not currently supported.
key_tradeoffs.provider_compatibility_vs_feature_covera: Only OpenAI and Cohere providers are supported for native token counting due to lack of standardization in other LLM providers. This tradeoff limits feature coverage but avoids complexity from handling multiple response schema variations.
key_tradeoffs.data_model_stability_vs_extraction_compl: The Answer metadata structure is kept stable with token usage stored as structured data under a predictable key, but this requires changes to the instrumentation flow since current hooks cannot access raw API responses containing the token data.

technology_considerations: OpenAI API response schema parsing for prompt_tokens and completion_tokens extraction
Cohere API response schema parsing for billed_units input_tokens and output_tokens extraction
Instrumentation system modification to capture raw API responses before Answer objects are created
LLM provider interface extension with optional chat_with_response method for token data access
TokenUsageData structured data model implementation to store prompt_tokens and completion_tokens
architecture.scope_statement: Modify LLM provider interfaces to optionally return API responses with usage data, update instrumentation system to capture these extended responses through a new chat_with_response method pattern, and implement proper token parsing logic in TokenTrackerHook based on standardized response schemas from OpenAI and Cohere providers.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Tracking via Instrumentation Hooks
adrs.context: The system needs to track LLM token usage for cost monitoring and optimization, but providers have inconsistent API response schemas for exposing this data.
adrs.decision: Implement token tracking through instrumentation hooks that parse API responses from supported providers (OpenAI, Cohere) when explicitly enabled via hook registration.
adrs.rationale: This approach maintains backward compatibility by making token tracking opt-in, allows graceful handling of non-tracking providers, and follows existing instrumentation patterns in the codebase. The design leverages the established BenchmarkHook protocol and Answer metadata structure for extensibility.
consequences: Performance impact only when hooks are registered
Token usage data becomes available through Answer.metadata['token_usage'] property
Only OpenAI and Cohere providers will include token data due to API response format differences
Requires modification of LLM provider interfaces to expose raw responses for extraction
alternatives_considered: Modify LLM provider wrappers to return structured token usage alongside text answers - rejected because it breaks existing APIs and requires extensive changes across multiple providers

Create a separate token tracking service that intercepts all LLM calls at HTTP client level - rejected because it would be more complex than the existing instrumentation system and introduce additional dependency management overhead

adrs.title: ADR: Optional Full Response Return vs. Mandatory Response Object
adrs.context: The architecture extends provider interfaces with an optional chat_with_response method that returns full API responses alongside text answers. This design choice affects what data is exposed across component boundaries and how failures are handled.
adrs.decision: Use optional extension methods rather than mandatory response objects to maintain backward compatibility while enabling token tracking capabilities.
adrs.rationale: This approach allows existing providers to continue working without modification while giving new/updated providers the capability to expose raw responses. The failure mode of missing token data is accepted as acceptable for legacy implementations, with clear documentation that token tracking requires provider updates.
consequences: Existing code using only chat() method continues to work unchanged
TokenTrackerHook must handle cases where full responses are not available by falling back to post-processing or skipping metrics collection
Provider implementation becomes more complex due to dual method signatures (chat + chat_with_response)
Backward compatibility is maintained but token tracking requires explicit provider support
alternatives_considered: Mandatory response objects that all providers must implement - would break existing integrations and force invasive changes
Separate metrics collection layer that parses answer strings for usage indicators - would be fragile and error-prone with no guarantee of accuracy
Pass-through raw responses through intermediate layers - would require significant architectural changes to propagate full API responses throughout the system

components.name: TokenTrackerHook
components.purpose: Instrumentation hook for tracking LLM token usage from API responses
responsibilities: Parse response objects to extract token usage data
Store extracted token data in Answer.metadata['token_usage']
Handle errors gracefully when providers don't return usage metadata
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: OpenAIChat
components.purpose: OpenAI provider wrapper for chat completion with optional response metadata extraction
responsibilities: Make HTTP calls to OpenAI API
Return chat responses with token usage data when available
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, base_url: str | None, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: CohereChat
components.purpose: Cohere provider wrapper for chat completion with optional response metadata extraction
responsibilities: Make HTTP calls to Cohere API
Return chat responses with token usage data when available
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

integration_points: OpenAIChat — chat method calls OpenAI API and returns string response; token usage data available in API response but lost during Answer wrapping
CohereChat — chat method calls Cohere API and returns string response; token usage data available in API response but lost during Answer wrapping
AnthropicChat — chat method calls Anthropic API and returns string response; does not expose token usage natively so no tracking possible
OllamaChat — chat method calls Ollama API and returns string response; does not expose token usage natively so no tracking possible

artifacts.content:
# Copyright (c) 2024 Fitz AI
"""
Token tracking instrumentation for LLM calls.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any, cast

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.core.answer import Answer

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

class TokenTrackerHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """
    Instrumentation hook for tracking LLM token usage from API responses.
    Currently supports OpenAI and Cohere providers that return usage metadata.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._call_contexts: dict[str, Any] = {}

    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        # Generate unique context ID for this call
        call_id = f"{layer}:{plugin_name}:{method}:{id(args)}"
        self._call_contexts[call_id] = {
            "layer": layer,
            "plugin_name": plugin_name,
            "method": method,
            "args": args,
            "kwargs": kwargs,
        }
        return call_id

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: str,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        # Only process successful chat calls with Answers
        if not isinstance(result, Answer):
            return

        # Get the call details from our context
        call_details = self._call_contexts.get(context)
        if not call_details:
            return

        try:
            layer = call_details["layer"]
            plugin_name = call_details["plugin_name"]
            method = call_details["method"]

            # Only track chat methods from supported providers that may have usage info
            if (method == "chat" and 
                (layer in ["openai", "cohere"])):
                self._process_token_usage(result, context)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(
                f"Failed to process token usage for {plugin_name}.{method}: {e}"
            )

    def _process_token_usage(self, answer: Answer, call_context_id: str) -> None:
        # This is a placeholder that would be enhanced by the actual response parsing.
        # In practice, this hook needs to receive API responses from providers,
        # not just final answers. We'll need to restructure to capture these in the
        # instrumented proxy logic or via a custom wrapper approach.
        pass
artifacts.purpose: Primary token tracking instrumentation hook that will parse and store usage data
Extends Answer class to provide a clean accessor for token usage information

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Implement TokenTrackerHook
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking to monitor LLM tokens consumed per query by instrumenting provider methods that consume tokens
deliverables: TokenTrackerHook class in fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py that intercepts LLM method calls and tracks input/output tokens
Enhanced instrumentation logic in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py with maybe_wrap_with_token_tracking function to register TokenTrackerHook for LLM providers
Updated provider classes (openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py) to apply token tracking wrapping during initialization
key_risks: Token extraction may fail for non-OpenAI providers that don't return standard usage fields
Hook registration timing issues if instrumentation happens before provider initialization
Performance overhead from additional logging in high-frequency LLM calls

risks.description: Token tracking hook fails to extract token usage from provider responses due to format inconsistencies across LLM providers
risks.mitigation: Implement comprehensive response parsing logic for each supported provider type and add fallback mechanisms
risks.contingency: Fall back to text length-based estimation when direct token counts are unavailable, log warnings for unsupported formats
risks.verification: Assert that all provider responses with usage fields are properly parsed

risks.description: Instrumentation system integration breaks existing functionality due to improper hook registration or method wrapping
risks.mitigation: Thoroughly test the instrumentation changes against all LLM provider implementations and ensure backward compatibility
risks.contingency: Rollback to previous instrumentation pattern with manual token tracking as fallback, revert hook registration logic
risks.verification: Assert that existing provider methods continue functioning correctly after wrapping

risks.description: LLM providers return malformed or missing usage data causing token tracking to fail silently
risks.mitigation: Add comprehensive error handling and validation in the token extraction logic with explicit logging of failures
risks.contingency: Implement fallback to approximate token estimation based on text length as secondary source when provider data is invalid
risks.verification: Assert that all token tracking errors are logged at warning level

risks.description: Token usage logging overwhelms system resources or creates performance bottlenecks during high-volume queries
risks.mitigation: Implement batching of token logs and limit log verbosity to debug level by default with configurable thresholds
risks.contingency: Switch logging mechanism to asynchronous buffering, implement rate limiting on token log entries
risks.verification: Assert that logging overhead does not exceed 5% of total query processing time under load

risks.description: Token tracking logs may inadvertently expose sensitive information or PII in query context
risks.mitigation: Sanitize log outputs and ensure token usage is logged separately from query content with strict access controls
risks.contingency: Implement mandatory log redaction rules for all tracked data, restrict access to token tracking logs via RBAC
risks.verification: Assert that no sensitive information appears in token tracking logs through automated security scanning

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback to approximate token estimation based on text length when provider data is invalid or unavailable
Rollback to previous instrumentation pattern with manual token tracking as fallback, revert hook registration logic if integration breaks existing functionality
Switch logging mechanism to asynchronous buffering and implement rate limiting on token log entries to prevent resource exhaustion
